name: Miami-Dade County Public Schools
about: Miami-Dade County Public Schools (M-DCPS) is the fourth largest school district in the United States, serving approximately 350,000 students across more than 500 schools in Miami-Dade County, Florida.
calendarNotes: Miami-Dade typically begins the school year in mid-August — roughly two weeks earlier than most of the country — and runs one of the longest instructional calendars in Florida. If you're planning family travel, Spring Break in Miami-Dade usually falls in mid-to-late March, sometimes a week later than neighboring Broward County, so check the calendar before booking. Winter Break runs about two weeks, and South Florida's warm weather makes it one of the busiest travel periods in the region. Families new to Miami-Dade should note that the official calendar is published in English, Spanish, and Haitian Creole on the district website and is approved annually by the school board — dates can shift slightly from year to year.
